preamble: Assibilation and voicing of [t] to [dz] typically occurs in intervocalic or front-vowel environments.

examples.note: Development of a voiced affricate from a voiceless stop.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
